findings.description: monte_carlo_p_hit() executes 50 Monte Carlo simulations with std::mt19937 RNG on hot path. Each simulation includes sqrt(), normal distribution sampling. Estimated: 50-200μs per call. Called from solve() which is on fire-control decision path.

solve_drop() contains 1000-iteration optimization loop searching for minimum velocity. Loop body includes sqrt(), atan2(). Worst-case: 1000 iterations × ~50ns = 50μs. Deterministic but significant budget consumption.
detect() calls cv::createCLAHE(2.0, {8,8}) on EVERY frame. CLAHE is computationally expensive histogram equalization. At 1536×864 resolution, this alone can exceed 2-3ms on RPi 5.
detect() calls orb_->detectAndCompute() on every frame. ORB feature detection at 1536×864 with 300 keypoints: estimated 3-5ms on RPi 5. This exceeds the 5ms WCET budget alone.
detect() calls matcher_->knnMatch() with k=2 for template matching. BFMatcher with NORM_HAMMING on 300 descriptors: O(n×m) complexity. Estimated 1-3ms depending on template count.

detect() calls cv::findHomography() with RANSAC (3.0 threshold, 8-point algorithm). RANSAC is iterative and non-deterministic. Worst-case: 5-10ms. This is the dominant WCET contributor in vision pipeline.
update() calls tracker_->update() which runs CSRT correlation tracking. CSRT is accurate but slow: 10-20ms at 1536×864 on RPi 5. This exceeds the 5ms budget by 2-4×.
update() calls cv::cvtColor() for redetect(). BGR2GRAY conversion at 1536×864: ~0.5-1ms. Not blocking alone but adds to cumulative budget.
redetect() calls cv::matchTemplate() with TM_CCOEFF_NORMED. Template matching is O(n×m) and slow. Search region dependent but can exceed 5ms for large ROIs.
frame_to_json() uses std::ostringstream for JSON serialization. Each call allocates internal string buffer. On broadcast path, this triggers heap allocation every frame.
send_to_clients() creates std::vector<int> dead for dead connection tracking. Heap allocation in client broadcast path.
start() uses std::stringstream for session filename generation. One-time allocation at startup, not on hot path.
log_frame() queues CsvLogEntry to std::queue. Entry is copied by value (trivially copyable), but queue node allocation may occur if capacity exceeded.

accept_loop() calls ::accept() in blocking mode. Server socket is O_NONBLOCK but accept() still returns -1/EAGAIN. Thread sleeps 5ms on no connection. Not on RT path but could cause priority inversion if thread priority not managed.
send_to_clients() calls ::send() with MSG_NOSIGNAL. While non-blocking socket won't block indefinitely, send() can still block on full socket buffer. No timeout specified.

trigger_effector() calls std::this_thread::sleep_for(pulse_ms). Blocking sleep in actuation path. If called from RT thread, this blocks the thread for pulse duration (default 10ms = 10 frame periods).
write_pwm_channel() calls ::write() to I2C device. I2C transactions can block for several ms waiting for bus availability. No timeout mechanism. On actuation hot path.
send_to_clients() acquires clients_mutex_ with std::lock_guard. Mutex held during send() syscall to all clients. If any client is slow, all other threads block waiting for mutex.
log_frame() acquires queue_mutex_ for queue operations. Mutex held during potential pop operations for backpressure. Low contention expected with single producer.
MPMCRingBuffer::push() and pop() use std::mutex for MPMC synchronization. Not lock-free. If used in RT path with multiple producers/consumers, contention will cause WCET violations.
CameraWrapper constructor uses std::make_unique<Impl>(). One-time allocation at camera initialization, not on frame processing hot path.
FrameBufferAllocator::allocate() calls buffers_.resize(). One-time allocation at camera setup, not on hot path.
add_template() calls orb_->detectAndCompute() and templates_.push_back(). Called during template registration (not hot path), but push_back can trigger vector reallocation.
capture_reference_template() calls bgr_frame(roi).clone(). This performs memcpy of ROI data. Called during template capture (not per-frame hot path).
CsvLogEntry::copy_to_array() uses strncpy for module/event strings. Small fixed-size copy, negligible impact.

vision_pipeline_120hz.verdict: BLOCKS_WCET - Vision pipeline exceeds 5ms budget by 2-3×

track_compute_120hz.verdict: BLOCKS_WCET - CSRT tracking exceeds 2ms budget by 5-10×

safety_monitor_1khz.verdict: PASS - Safety monitor uses atomic operations and simple comparisons

recommendations.issue: ORB detector + RANSAC homography too slow for 120Hz
recommendations.solution: Replace with faster detector (AprilTag, ArUco) or reduce resolution. Consider hardware-accelerated vision (OpenCV with GStreamer/V4L2 M2M).

recommendations.issue: CSRT tracker too slow for 2ms budget
recommendations.solution: Use KCF or MOSSE tracker (10× faster, slightly less accurate). Or run tracker at reduced rate (60Hz) with interpolation.

recommendations.issue: Monte Carlo P_hit calculation on hot path
recommendations.solution: Pre-compute lookup table for P_hit vs range/elevation. Or use analytical approximation instead of 50-sample Monte Carlo.

recommendations.issue: I2C write blocking on actuation path
recommendations.solution: Use async I2C with DMA. Or move I2C to dedicated thread with shared memory command buffer.
Cache CLAHE object, apply only when lighting changes detected. Or use simpler gamma correction.

recommendations.issue: Heap allocation in HUD socket broadcast
recommendations.solution: Use pre-allocated char buffer with snprintf for JSON. Reserve vector capacity for client list.
Use LockFreeRingBuffer (SPSC) for single-producer single-consumer paths. Already available in ring_buffer.hpp.
